free_text: Inelastic scattering and reaction data analysis for the
3He+44,48Ca systems
Elast. scatt. and description
of experiment
The detector system for the ejectives consisted
of dE-E telescopes mounted on two moveable arms at
equal angles on either side of the beam axis. Each arm
had three telescopes separated by a nominal spacing of
10 degr. for the first (44Ca) experiment and 5 degr.
for the second experiment. For all telescopes, the
collimating apertures had an area of 34.3+-1.5 mm**2.
The distances to the detector collimation apertures for
the two experiments were 199.5+-0.3 mm and 300.7+-0.3
mm.
44Ca and 48Ca pure self-supporting targets. The first
experiment, involving 3He+44Ca, used a target of
3.2 mg/cm2 thickness. However, due to a lower than
expected beam current, resulting in only 100-200 pA
instead of around 1.0 nA, this target was doubled to
6.4 mg/cm2 for part of the experiment to increase the
yield. The second experiment used a 48Ca target of
5.6 mg/cm2 thickness, with a beam current in the region
of 1.0 nA.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Subents 022-025 added.
